---
title: "Diabetes Burden in US-Mexico Border States using GBD 2021"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    df-print: paged
    highlight: pygments
    theme: cerulean
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    number-sections: false
    toc-float: 
      collapsed: false
      smooth-scroll: false
execute:
  echo: true
css: styles.css
date: today
---

## Setup

```{r paths-config, include=FALSE}
# ── Project paths ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# All paths are resolved relative to the project root using {here}.
# Place a blank `.here` file (or an `.Rproj` file) at the repo root so
# here::here() always anchors correctly regardless of working directory.

library(here)

# Processed data (produced by File 1)
path_proc <- here("data", "processed")
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

#| include: false

# Source IHME GBD 2021
# install.packages("janitor")
# install.packages("kableExtra")
# install.packages("rnaturalearthdata")
# install.packages("tidyverse")

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(janitor)
library(ggplot2)
library(kableExtra)
library(scales)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

library(rnaturalearth)
library(rnaturalearthdata)

library(spdep)
library(sf)
library(leaflet)


library(viridis)

library(patchwork)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggspatial)

library(rnaturalearth)
library(rnaturalearthdata)

# Set theme for plots
theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12))
```

## Maps: Spatial Autocorrelation and Clustering Analysis

### Crude Mortality Rate

#### State

```{r spatial1, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#| label: spatial-clustering
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10


states_map_data_USMX <- readRDS(file.path(path_proc, "map_data_states_USMX.rds"))

# Advanced spatial analysis for state
  # Merge spatial and diabetes data
#   merged_data <-states_map_data_USMX %>%
#     left_join(
#       df %>%
#         filter(
#           year == 2021,
#           metric_name == "Rate",
#           age_name == "All ages",
#           sex_name == "Both",
#           cause_name== "Overall",
#           measure_name == "Prevalence"
#         ) %>% 
#       mutate(
#     # Standardize location names for matching
#     location_clean = case_when(
#       location_name == "Nuevo León" ~ "Nuevo Leon",
#       location_name == "Nuevo LeÃ³n" ~ "Nuevo Leon",
#       TRUE ~ location_name
#     )
#   ) %>%
#   rename(
#     COUNTRY = country,
#     STATE_NAME = location_name
#   ),
#       by = c("STATE_NAME", "COUNTRY")
#     )
#   
#   # Create spatial weights matrix
# filtered_data <- merged_data[!is.na(merged_data$val), ]
# 
# coords <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(filtered_data,of_largest_polygon = TRUE))
# nb <- knn2nb(knearneigh(coords, k = 1))
# weights <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")
#   
#   # Calculate spatial autocorrelation
#   moran_test <- moran.test(filtered_data$val, weights, zero.policy = TRUE, na.action = na.omit)
#   
#   # print(moran_test)
#   
#   # Local indicators of spatial association (LISA)
#   lisa <- localmoran(filtered_data$val, weights, na.action = na.exclude, zero.policy = TRUE)
#   
#   lag_val <- lag.listw(weights, filtered_data$val, zero.policy = TRUE, NAOK = TRUE)
#   mean_val <- mean(filtered_data$val, na.rm = TRUE)
# 
#   # Assign basic cluster type
#   cluster_type <- ifelse(
#     filtered_data$val > mean_val & lag_val > mean_val, "High-High",
#     ifelse(filtered_data$val < mean_val & lag_val < mean_val, "Low-Low",
#     ifelse(filtered_data$val < mean_val & lag_val > mean_val, "Low-High", "High-Low"))
#   )
#   
#   # Combine with significance
#   filtered_data$lisa_cluster <- ifelse(
#     lisa[,5] < 0.05,
#     paste0(cluster_type, " (p < 0.05)"),
#     paste0(cluster_type, " (NS)")
#   )
#   
#   # Convert to factor
#   filtered_data$lisa_cluster <- as.factor(filtered_data$lisa_cluster)
#   
#   # Getis-Ord hot spot analysis
# getis_ord <- localG(filtered_data$val, weights)
# # print(getis_ord)
# 
# filtered_data$hotspot <- as.factor(
#   ifelse(getis_ord > 1.96, "Hot Spot (95%)",
#          ifelse(getis_ord > 1.28, "Hot Spot (80%)",
#                 ifelse(getis_ord < -1.28, "Cold Spot (80%)",
#                        ifelse(getis_ord < -1.96, "Cold Spot (95%)",
#                               "Not Significant"))))
# )
```

#### County

```{r spatial1, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
######################################################################################
# State-level spatial correlation doesn't exist. Continue with County-level analysis
######################################################################################

load("joined_diabetes_map_county_data.RData")
# counties_map_data_USMX <-joined_diabetes_map_county_data

# Advanced spatial analysis for counties
# Create spatial weights matrix
filtered_data_ct <- joined_diabetes_map_county_data[!is.na(joined_diabetes_map_county_data$`Crude Rate`), ]

coords <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(filtered_data_ct,of_largest_polygon = TRUE))
nb <- knn2nb(knearneigh(coords, k = 3))
weights <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")
  
  # Calculate spatial autocorrelation using global Moran's I test
  moran_test <- moran.test(filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate`, weights, zero.policy = TRUE, na.action = na.omit)
  
  print(moran_test)

  
  # Local indicators of spatial association (LISA)
  lisa <- localmoran(filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate`, weights, na.action = na.exclude, zero.policy = TRUE)
  lag_val <- lag.listw(weights, filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate`, zero.policy = TRUE, NAOK = TRUE)
  mean_val <- mean(filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Add results to your spatial data
filtered_data_ct$local_moran_i <- lisa[, "Ii"]          # Local Moran's I statistic
filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <- lisa[, "Pr(z != E(Ii))"]  # p-values
filtered_data_ct$local_moran_z <- lisa[, "Z.Ii"]       # Z-scores


# Create significance categories
filtered_data_ct$significance <- case_when(
  filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <= 0.05 ~ "Significant",
  TRUE ~ "Not significant"
)

# Create LISA (Local Indicators of Spatial Association) categories
# This identifies High-High, Low-Low, High-Low, Low-High clusters
filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag <- lag.listw(weights, filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate`, zero.policy = TRUE)
filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_mean <- mean(filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE)
filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag_mean <- mean(filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag, na.rm = TRUE)

filtered_data_ct$lisa_category <- case_when(
  filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <= 0.05 & 
    filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate` > filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_mean & 
    filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag > filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag_mean ~ "High-High",
  filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <= 0.05 & 
    filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate` < filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_mean & 
    filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag < filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag_mean ~ "Low-Low",
  filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <= 0.05 & 
    filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate` > filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_mean & 
    filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag < filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag_mean ~ "High-Low",
  filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <= 0.05 & 
    filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate` < filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_mean & 
    filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag > filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag_mean ~ "Low-High",
  TRUE ~ "Not significant"
)

# Assign basic cluster type
  cluster_type <- ifelse(
    filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate` > mean_val & lag_val > mean_val, "High-High",
    ifelse(filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate` < mean_val & lag_val < mean_val, "Low-Low",
    ifelse(filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate` < mean_val & lag_val > mean_val, "Low-High", "High-Low"))
  )
  
# Combine with significance
  filtered_data_ct$lisa_cluster <- ifelse(
    lisa[,5] < 0.05,
    paste0(cluster_type, " (p < 0.05)"),
    paste0(cluster_type, " (NS)")
  )
  
# Convert to factor
  filtered_data_ct$lisa_cluster <- as.factor(filtered_data_ct$lisa_cluster)
  
# Getis-Ord hot spot analysis
filtered_data_ct$getis_ord <- localG(filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate`, weights)
# print(getis_ord)

filtered_data_ct$hotspot <- as.factor(
  ifelse(filtered_data_ct$getis_ord > 1.96, "Hot Spot (95%)",
         ifelse(filtered_data_ct$getis_ord > 1.28, "Hot Spot (80%)",
                ifelse(filtered_data_ct$getis_ord < -1.28, "Cold Spot (80%)",
                       ifelse(filtered_data_ct$getis_ord < -1.96, "Cold Spot (95%)",
                              "Not Significant"))))
)


filtered_data_ct <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  mutate(LISA_simple = case_when(
    grepl("^High-High.*", lisa_cluster) ~ "High-High",  # Starts with High-High, followed by anything
    grepl("^Low-Low.*", lisa_cluster) ~ "Low-Low",
    grepl("^High-Low.*", lisa_cluster) ~ "High-Low",
    grepl("^Low-High.*", lisa_cluster) ~ "Low-High",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
  ))

# Create conceptual clustering visualization
clustering_concept <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  mutate(
    burden_category = case_when(
      `Crude Rate` >= quantile(`Crude Rate`, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "High Burden",
      `Crude Rate` >= quantile(`Crude Rate`, 0.5, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Moderate-High Burden",
      `Crude Rate` >= quantile(`Crude Rate`, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Moderate-Low Burden",
      TRUE ~ "Low Burden"
    ),
    cluster_risk = case_when(
      COUNTRY == "Mexico" & `Crude Rate` > median(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "High-Risk Cluster",
      COUNTRY == "USA" & `Crude Rate` < median(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Low-Risk Cluster",
      TRUE ~ "Mixed Pattern"
    )
  )

# Clean problematic characters in county names
clustering_concept$COUNTY_NAME <- iconv(clustering_concept$COUNTY_NAME, 
                                       from = "latin1", to = "UTF-8", 
                                       sub = "")
# # create plot 
# clustering_plot <- ggplot(clustering_concept, aes(x = reorder(COUNTY_NAME, `Crude Rate`), y = `Crude Rate`)) +
#   geom_col(aes(fill = burden_category), alpha = 0.8) +
#   geom_text(aes(label = round(`Crude Rate`, 0)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
#   scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma") +
#   scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +  # Add 15% space on right
#   coord_flip() +
#   labs(
#     title = "Diabetes Mortality Burden Clustering Pattern (2018-2023)",
#     subtitle = "Identification of high and low burden clusters for targeted intervention",
#     x = "USA MEXICO Border States",
#     y = "Prevalence Rate per 100,000",
#     fill = "Burden Category"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal()

# print(clustering_plot)




# Map 1: Conceptual clustering

# Non-spatial classification - only uses crude rate values
# Top 25% = High Burden, Next 25% = Moderate-High, etc.

#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10

# Get country boundaries for US and Mexico
countries <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") %>%
  filter(admin %in% c("United States of America", "Mexico"))

# Get state/province boundaries for both countries
states_us <- ne_states(country = "United States of America", returnclass = "sf")
states_mexico <- ne_states(country = "Mexico", returnclass = "sf")

# Combine state boundaries
all_states <- rbind(
  states_us %>% select(name, admin, geometry),
  states_mexico %>% select(name, admin, geometry)
)

# Create a more precise border line
border_line <- st_intersection(
  st_boundary(countries %>% filter(admin == "United States of America")),
  st_boundary(countries %>% filter(admin == "Mexico"))
)

# Create a buffer around the border line
border_buffer <- st_buffer(border_line, dist = 0.05) 

# Color by burden category instead of continuous rate
burden_map <- ggplot() +
  # Add country boundaries as base layer
  geom_sf(data = countries, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "gray60", size = 0.3) +
  
  # Add your diabetes data on top
  geom_sf(data = clustering_concept, aes(fill = burden_category), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  
  # Highlighted border
  geom_sf(data = border_buffer, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
    
  scale_fill_viridis_d(
    name = "Diabetes Mortality Rate\n(deaths per 100,000)",
    option = "plasma",
    direction = -1,
    na.value = "grey90"
    ) +

    # Focus on border region
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-125, -92),  # Adjust longitude range as needed
    ylim = c(22, 42),     # Adjust latitude range as needed
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
    
    labs(title = "Diabetes Mortality Burden Categories",
         subtitle = "County-level clustering analysis (2018-2023)") +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 20)), 
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.key.width = unit(1.5, "cm"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5, nrow = 1))


# print(burden_map)- ignore this as no Non-spatial and non-statistical classification

######################################################################################
# LISA map (most informative): Local Indicator of Spatial Association
######################################################################################
LISA_map_cdr <- ggplot() +
  # Add country boundaries as base layer
  geom_sf(data = countries, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "gray60", size = 0.3) +

  geom_sf(data=clustering_concept, aes(fill = LISA_simple), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("High-High" = "#d73027",    # Red: High diabetes surrounded by high
               "Low-Low" = "#4575b4",      # Blue: Low diabetes surrounded by low
               "High-Low" = "#f46d43",     # Orange: High diabetes surrounded by low
               "Low-High" = "#74add1",     # Light blue: Low diabetes surrounded by high
               "Not significant" = "#e0e0e0"), # Gray: Not significant
    name = "LISA Category"
  ) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.3) +
  
  # Highlighted border
  geom_sf(data = border_buffer, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  
   # Focus on border region
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-125, -92),  # Adjust longitude range as needed
    ylim = c(22, 42),     # Adjust latitude range as needed
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Spatial Clusters of Diabetes Mortality Crude Rates",
    subtitle = "Local Indicators of Spatial Association (LISA) Map"
  ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 20)), 
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.key.width = unit(1.5, "cm"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5, nrow = 1))

print(LISA_map_cdr)

ggsave("cdr_county_lisa_map.png", LISA_map_cdr, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, units = "in", bg = "white")

# LISA chart
LISA_Chart <- ggplot(clustering_concept, aes(x = `Crude Rate`, y = crude_rate_lag)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = LISA_simple, size = significance), alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean_val, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", size = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_val, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", size = 0.8) +
  
  # Enhanced titles and labels
  labs(
    title = "Moran Scatterplot: Crude Rate vs. Spatial Lag",
    subtitle = "Local Indicators of Spatial Association (LISA) Chart",
    x = "Crude Rate (per 100,000)",
    y = "Spatial Lag of Crude Rate",
    caption = "Data: CDC, INEGI",
    color = "LISA Category",
    size = "Statistical Significance"
  ) +
  
  # Improved color scheme for LISA categories
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "LISA Category",
    values = c(
      "High-High" = "#d73027",      # Red for hotspots
      "Low-Low" = "#1a9850",        # Green for coldspots  
      "High-Low" = "#fdae61",       # Orange for outliers
      "Low-High" = "#abd9e9",       # Light blue for outliers
      "Not Significant" = "#969696"  # Gray for non-significant
    ),
    labels = c(
      "High-High" = "High-High (Hotspot)",
      "Low-Low" = "Low-Low (Coldspot)", 
      "High-Low" = "High-Low (Outlier)",
      "Low-High" = "Low-High (Outlier)",
      "Not Significant" = "Not Significant"
    )
  ) +
  
  # Improved size scale
  scale_size_manual(
    name = "Statistical\nSignificance",
    values = c("Significant" = 2.5, "Not significant" = 1.2),
    labels = c("Significant" = "Significant (p ≤ 0.05)", "Not significant" = "Not Significant")
  ) +
  
  # Enhanced theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40"),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray50", hjust = 0),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "gray80", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
  ) +
  
  # Add quadrant labels for interpretation
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = "HH", 
           hjust = 1.2, vjust = 1.2, size = 4, color = "gray60", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "LH", 
           hjust = -0.2, vjust = 1.2, size = 4, color = "gray60", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = -Inf, label = "LL", 
           hjust = -0.2, vjust = -0.2, size = 4, color = "gray60", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = -Inf, label = "HL", 
           hjust = 1.2, vjust = -0.2, size = 4, color = "gray60", fontface = "bold")

# Display the chart
print(LISA_Chart)

ggsave("LISA_Chart.png", LISA_Chart, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, units = "in", bg = "white")

######################################################################################
# hotspot map using Getis-Ord statistic
######################################################################################

cdr_getisrdg_map <-
ggplot() +
  # Add country boundaries as base layer
  geom_sf(data = countries, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "gray60", size = 0.3) +
  
  geom_sf(data=filtered_data_ct, aes(fill = hotspot), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Hot Spot (95%)" = "red",
    "Hot Spot (80%)" = "orange",
    "Cold Spot (80%)" = "lightblue", 
    "Cold Spot (95%)" = "blue",
    "Not Significant" = "gray"
  ),  na.value = "white") +
    # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.3) +
  
  # Highlighted border
  geom_sf(data = border_buffer, fill = "white", color = "black", size= 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  
   # Focus on border region
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-125, -92),  # Adjust longitude range as needed
    ylim = c(22, 42),     # Adjust latitude range as needed
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Spatial Distribution of Hot and Cold Spots Map",
       subtitle = "Crude Mortality Rate using Getis-Ord statistic",
       fill = "Hotspot Type"
       ) + 
      theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 20)), 
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.key.width = unit(1.5, "cm"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm"),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5, nrow = 1))


print(cdr_getisrdg_map)

ggsave("cdr_getisrdg_map.png", cdr_getisrdg_map, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, units = "in", bg = "white")

######################################################################################
#Categories of Crude rate
######################################################################################

# Load the RData file
load("joined_diabetes_map_county_data.RData")

data<- joined_diabetes_map_county_data

# Filter for border states
# US border states: California, Arizona, New Mexico, Texas
# Mexico border states: Baja California, Sonora, Chihuahua, Coahuila, Nuevo León, Tamaulipas

border_states_us <- c("California", "Arizona", "New Mexico", "Texas")
border_states_mx <- c("Baja California", "Sonora", "Chihuahua", "Coahuila", 
                      "Nuevo León", "Tamaulipas")

# Filter data for border region
border_data <- data %>%
  filter(STATE_NAME %in% c(border_states_us, border_states_mx))

# Create categories for mapping
border_data <- border_data %>%
  mutate(
    # Clean crude rate variable (handle any text/character issues)
    crude_rate_clean = as.numeric(`Crude Rate`),
    # Create rate categories (diabetes mortality per 100,000)
    rate_category = case_when(
      crude_rate_clean < 25 ~ "< 25 (Low)",
      crude_rate_clean >= 25 & crude_rate_clean < 50 ~ "25-50 (Moderate)",
      crude_rate_clean >= 50 & crude_rate_clean < 100 ~ "50-100 (High)",
      crude_rate_clean >= 100 & crude_rate_clean < 150 ~ "100-150 (Very High)",
      crude_rate_clean >= 150 ~ "≥ 150 (Extremely High)",
      TRUE ~ "No Data"
    ),
    # Create border status
    # border_status = ifelse(is_us_border == 1, "Border County", "Non-Border County"),
    # Country indicator
    country_clean = case_when(
      COUNTRY == "US" | COUNTRY == "USA" ~ "United States",
      COUNTRY == "MX" | COUNTRY == "Mexico" ~ "Mexico",
      TRUE ~ COUNTRY
    )
  )

# Set factor levels for consistent coloring
border_data$rate_category <- factor(border_data$rate_category, 
                                   levels = c("(Very Low)", "< 25 (Low)", "25-50 (Moderate)", "50-100 (High)", 
                                             "100-150 (Very High)", "≥ 150 (Extremely High)", "No Data"))

print(paste("Total counties in border region:", nrow(border_data)))
print("Summary of crude rates:")

border_data %>%
  filter(crude_rate_clean != 9999) %>%
  pull(crude_rate_clean) %>%
  summary()

# Summary statistics by country and border status
summary_stats <- border_data %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(!is.na(crude_rate_clean)) %>%
  group_by(country_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    n_counties = n(),
    mean_rate = round(mean(crude_rate_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    median_rate = round(median(crude_rate_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    min_rate = round(min(crude_rate_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    max_rate = round(max(crude_rate_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(summary_stats, caption = "Diabetes Mortality Rate Summary by Country")

#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10


# Get country boundaries for US and Mexico
countries <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") %>%
  filter(admin %in% c("United States of America", "Mexico"))

# Get state/province boundaries for both countries
states_us <- ne_states(country = "United States of America", returnclass = "sf")
states_mexico <- ne_states(country = "Mexico", returnclass = "sf")

# Combine state boundaries
all_states <- rbind(
  states_us %>% select(name, admin, geometry),
  states_mexico %>% select(name, admin, geometry)
)

# Create a more precise border line
border_line <- st_intersection(
  st_boundary(countries %>% filter(admin == "United States of America")),
  st_boundary(countries %>% filter(admin == "Mexico"))
)

# Create a buffer around the border line
border_buffer <- st_buffer(border_line, dist = 0.05) 

# Create the map
main_map <- ggplot() +
  # Add country boundaries as base layer
  geom_sf(data = countries, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "gray60", size = 0.3) +
  
  # Add your diabetes data on top
  geom_sf(data = border_data, aes(fill = rate_category), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  
  # ADD STATE BOUNDARIES ON TOP - this is the key addition
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Highlighted border
  geom_sf(data = border_buffer, fill = "black", color = "black", size = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # scale_fill_viridis_d(
  #   name = "Diabetes Mortality Rate\n(deaths per 100,000)",
  #   option = "plasma",
  #   direction = -1,
  #   na.value = "grey90"
  # ) +
  
    scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Diabetes Mortality Rate\n(deaths per 100,000)",
    values = c(
    "< 25 (Low)" = "#fee5d9",
    "25-50 (Moderate)" = "#fcbba1", 
    "50-100 (High)" = "#fc9272",
    "100-150 (Very High)" = "#fb6a4a",
    "≥ 150 (Extremely High)" = "#de2d26",
    "No Data" = "#fef5f0"
    )) +
  
  # Focus on border region
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-125, -92),  # Adjust longitude range as needed
    ylim = c(22, 42),     # Adjust latitude range as needed
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Diabetes Mortality Rate by County",
    subtitle = "US-Mexico Border Region",
    caption = paste("Data covers", "2018-2023", "| Source: INEGI, CONAPO, CDC")
  ) +
  
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.key.width = unit(1.5, "cm"),
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
  ) +
  
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5, nrow = 1))

print(main_map)

ggsave("cdr_county_map.png", main_map, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, units = "in", bg = "white")

```

### Age-standardized Mortality Rate
#### State
```{r}
#| label: spatial-clustering
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10

# State-Level
```

#### County
```{r}
######################################################################################
# Continue with County-level analysis
######################################################################################

load("joined_asdmr_map_county_data.RData")

# Advanced spatial analysis for counties
# Create spatial weights matrix
filtered_data_ct <- joined_asdmr_map_county_data[!is.na(joined_asdmr_map_county_data$`asmr_per_100k`), ]

coords <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(filtered_data_ct,of_largest_polygon = TRUE))
nb <- knn2nb(knearneigh(coords, k = 3))
weights <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")
  
  # Calculate spatial autocorrelation using global Moran's I test
  moran_test <- moran.test(filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k`, weights, zero.policy = TRUE, na.action = na.omit)
  
  print(moran_test)

  
  # Local indicators of spatial association (LISA)
  lisa <- localmoran(filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k`, weights, na.action = na.exclude, zero.policy = TRUE)
  lag_val <- lag.listw(weights, filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k`, zero.policy = TRUE, NAOK = TRUE)
  mean_val <- mean(filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k`, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Add results to your spatial data
filtered_data_ct$local_moran_i <- lisa[, "Ii"]          # Local Moran's I statistic
filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <- lisa[, "Pr(z != E(Ii))"]  # p-values
filtered_data_ct$local_moran_z <- lisa[, "Z.Ii"]       # Z-scores


# Create significance categories
filtered_data_ct$significance <- case_when(
  filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <= 0.05 ~ "Significant",
  TRUE ~ "Not significant"
)

# Create LISA (Local Indicators of Spatial Association) categories
# This identifies High-High, Low-Low, High-Low, Low-High clusters
filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag <- lag.listw(weights, filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k`, zero.policy = TRUE)
filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_mean <- mean(filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k`, na.rm = TRUE)
filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag_mean <- mean(filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag, na.rm = TRUE)

filtered_data_ct$lisa_category <- case_when(
  filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <= 0.05 & 
    filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k` > filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_mean & 
    filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag > filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag_mean ~ "High-High",
  filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <= 0.05 & 
    filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k` < filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_mean & 
    filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag < filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag_mean ~ "Low-Low",
  filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <= 0.05 & 
    filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k` > filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_mean & 
    filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag < filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag_mean ~ "High-Low",
  filtered_data_ct$local_moran_p <= 0.05 & 
    filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k` < filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_mean & 
    filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag > filtered_data_ct$crude_rate_lag_mean ~ "Low-High",
  TRUE ~ "Not significant"
)

# Assign basic cluster type
  cluster_type <- ifelse(
    filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k` > mean_val & lag_val > mean_val, "High-High",
    ifelse(filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k` < mean_val & lag_val < mean_val, "Low-Low",
    ifelse(filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k` < mean_val & lag_val > mean_val, "Low-High", "High-Low"))
  )
  
# Combine with significance
  filtered_data_ct$lisa_cluster <- ifelse(
    lisa[,5] < 0.05,
    paste0(cluster_type, " (p < 0.05)"),
    paste0(cluster_type, " (NS)")
  )
  
# Convert to factor
  filtered_data_ct$lisa_cluster <- as.factor(filtered_data_ct$lisa_cluster)
  
# Getis-Ord hot spot analysis
filtered_data_ct$getis_ord <- localG(filtered_data_ct$`asmr_per_100k`, weights)
# print(getis_ord)

filtered_data_ct$hotspot <- as.factor(
  ifelse(filtered_data_ct$getis_ord > 1.96, "Hot Spot (95%)",
         ifelse(filtered_data_ct$getis_ord > 1.28, "Hot Spot (80%)",
                ifelse(filtered_data_ct$getis_ord < -1.28, "Cold Spot (80%)",
                       ifelse(filtered_data_ct$getis_ord < -1.96, "Cold Spot (95%)",
                              "Not Significant"))))
)


filtered_data_ct <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  mutate(LISA_simple = case_when(
    grepl("^High-High.*", lisa_cluster) ~ "High-High",  # Starts with High-High, followed by anything
    grepl("^Low-Low.*", lisa_cluster) ~ "Low-Low",
    grepl("^High-Low.*", lisa_cluster) ~ "High-Low",
    grepl("^Low-High.*", lisa_cluster) ~ "Low-High",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
  ))

save(filtered_data_ct, file = "filtered_data_ct_asmr.RData")

# Create conceptual clustering visualization
clustering_concept <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  mutate(
    burden_category = case_when(
      `asmr_per_100k` >= quantile(`asmr_per_100k`, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "High Burden",
      `asmr_per_100k` >= quantile(`asmr_per_100k`, 0.5, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Moderate-High Burden",
      `asmr_per_100k` >= quantile(`asmr_per_100k`, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Moderate-Low Burden",
      TRUE ~ "Low Burden"
    ),
    cluster_risk = case_when(
      COUNTRY == "Mexico" & `asmr_per_100k` > median(`asmr_per_100k`, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "High-Risk Cluster",
      COUNTRY == "USA" & `asmr_per_100k` < median(`asmr_per_100k`, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Low-Risk Cluster",
      TRUE ~ "Mixed Pattern"
    )
  )

# Clean problematic characters in county names
clustering_concept$COUNTY_NAME <- iconv(clustering_concept$COUNTY_NAME, 
                                       from = "latin1", to = "UTF-8", 
                                       sub = "")
# # create plot 
# clustering_plot <- ggplot(clustering_concept, aes(x = reorder(COUNTY_NAME, `asmr_per_100k`), y = `asmr_per_100k`)) +
#   geom_col(aes(fill = burden_category), alpha = 0.8) +
#   geom_text(aes(label = round(`asmr_per_100k`, 0)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
#   scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma") +
#   scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +  # Add 15% space on right
#   coord_flip() +
#   labs(
#     title = "Diabetes Mortality Burden Clustering Pattern (2018-2023)",
#     subtitle = "Identification of high and low burden clusters for targeted intervention",
#     x = "USA MEXICO Border States",
#     y = "Prevalence Rate per 100,000",
#     fill = "Burden Category"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal()

# print(clustering_plot)




# Map 1: Conceptual clustering

# Non-spatial classification - only uses asmr_per_100k values
# Top 25% = High Burden, Next 25% = Moderate-High, etc.

#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10

# Get country boundaries for US and Mexico
countries <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") %>%
  filter(admin %in% c("United States of America", "Mexico"))

# Get state/province boundaries for both countries
states_us <- ne_states(country = "United States of America", returnclass = "sf")
states_mexico <- ne_states(country = "Mexico", returnclass = "sf")

# Combine state boundaries
all_states <- rbind(
  states_us %>% select(name, admin, geometry),
  states_mexico %>% select(name, admin, geometry)
)

# Create a more precise border line
border_line <- st_intersection(
  st_boundary(countries %>% filter(admin == "United States of America")),
  st_boundary(countries %>% filter(admin == "Mexico"))
)

# Create a buffer around the border line
border_buffer <- st_buffer(border_line, dist = 0.05) 

# Color by burden category instead of continuous rate
asmr_burden_map <- ggplot() +
  # Add country boundaries as base layer
  geom_sf(data = countries, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "gray60", size = 0.3) +
  
  # Add your diabetes data on top
  geom_sf(data = clustering_concept, aes(fill = burden_category), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  
  # Highlighted border
  geom_sf(data = border_buffer, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
    
  scale_fill_viridis_d(
    name = "Diabetes Mortality Rate\n(deaths per 100,000)",
    option = "plasma",
    direction = -1,
    na.value = "grey90"
    ) +

    # Focus on border region
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-125, -90),  # Adjust longitude range as needed
    ylim = c(20, 40),     # Adjust latitude range as needed
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
    
    labs(title = "Diabetes Mortality Burden Categories",
         subtitle = "County-level clustering analysis (2018-2023)") +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 20)), 
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.key.width = unit(1.5, "cm"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm")
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5, nrow = 1))

asmr_burden_map
# print(burden_map)- ignore this as no Non-spatial and non-statistical classification

######################################################################################
# LISA: Local Indicator of Spatial Association
######################################################################################

# LISA map (most informative)
LISA_map <- ggplot() +
  # Add country boundaries as base layer
  geom_sf(data = countries, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "gray60", size = 0.3) +

  geom_sf(data=clustering_concept, aes(fill = LISA_simple), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("High-High" = "#d73027",    # Red: High diabetes surrounded by high
               "Low-Low" = "#4575b4",      # Blue: Low diabetes surrounded by low
               "High-Low" = "#f46d43",     # Orange: High diabetes surrounded by low
               "Low-High" = "#74add1",     # Light blue: Low diabetes surrounded by high
               "Not significant" = "#e0e0e0"), # Gray: Not significant
    name = "LISA Category"
  ) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.3) +
  
  # Highlighted border
  geom_sf(data = border_buffer, fill = "black", color = "black", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  
   # Focus on border region
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-125, -92),  # Adjust longitude range as needed
    ylim = c(22, 42),     # Adjust latitude range as needed
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Spatial Clusters of Age-Standardized Diabetes Mortality Rate",
    subtitle = "Local Indicators of Spatial Association (LISA) Map"
  ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 20)), 
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.key.width = unit(1.5, "cm"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm"),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5, nrow = 1))

print(LISA_map)

ggsave("asmr_county_lisa_map.png", LISA_map, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

######################################

# LISA chart
LISA_Chart <- ggplot(clustering_concept, aes(x = `asmr_per_100k`, y = crude_rate_lag)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = LISA_simple, size = significance), alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean_val, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", size = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_val, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", size = 0.8) +
  
  # Enhanced titles and labels
  labs(
    title = "Moran Scatterplot: ASMR per 100k vs. Spatial Lag",
    subtitle = "Local Indicators of Spatial Association (LISA) Chart",
    x = "Age-Standardized Diabetes Mortality Rate (per 100,000)",
    y = "Spatial Lag of ASMR",
    caption = "Data: CDC, INEGI",
    color = "LISA Category",
    size = "Statistical Significance"
  ) +
  
  # Improved color scheme for LISA categories
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "LISA Category",
    values = c(
      "High-High" = "#d73027",      # Red for hotspots
      "Low-Low" = "#1a9850",        # Green for coldspots  
      "High-Low" = "#fdae61",       # Orange for outliers
      "Low-High" = "#abd9e9",       # Light blue for outliers
      "Not Significant" = "#969696"  # Gray for non-significant
    ),
    labels = c(
      "High-High" = "High-High (Hotspot)",
      "Low-Low" = "Low-Low (Coldspot)", 
      "High-Low" = "High-Low (Outlier)",
      "Low-High" = "Low-High (Outlier)",
      "Not Significant" = "Not Significant"
    )
  ) +
  
  # Improved size scale
  scale_size_manual(
    name = "Statistical\nSignificance",
    values = c("Significant" = 2.5, "Not significant" = 1.2),
    labels = c("Significant" = "Significant (p ≤ 0.05)", "Not significant" = "Not Significant")
  ) +
  
  # Enhanced theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40"),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray50", hjust = 0),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "gray80", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
  ) +
  
  # Add quadrant labels for interpretation
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = "HH", 
           hjust = 1.2, vjust = 1.2, size = 4, color = "gray60", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "LH", 
           hjust = -0.2, vjust = 1.2, size = 4, color = "gray60", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = -Inf, label = "LL", 
           hjust = -0.2, vjust = -0.2, size = 4, color = "gray60", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = -Inf, label = "HL", 
           hjust = 1.2, vjust = -0.2, size = 4, color = "gray60", fontface = "bold")

# Display the chart
print(LISA_Chart)

ggsave("asmr_LISA_Chart.png", LISA_Chart, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, units = "in", bg = "white")


######################################################################################
# hotspot map using Getis-Ord statistic
######################################################################################

# First, transform your data to lat/lon
clustering_concept_latlon <- st_transform(clustering_concept, crs = 4326)
# Verify the new bounds
cat("New bounds in lat/lon:\n")
print(st_bbox(clustering_concept_latlon))

getisrdg_map <-
ggplot() +
  # Add country boundaries as base layer
  geom_sf(data = countries, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "gray60", size = 0.3) +
  
  geom_sf(data=clustering_concept_latlon, aes(fill = hotspot), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Hot Spot (95%)" = "red",
    "Hot Spot (80%)" = "orange",
    "Cold Spot (80%)" = "lightblue", 
    "Cold Spot (95%)" = "blue",
    "Not Significant" = "gray"
  ),  na.value = "white") +
    # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.3) +
  
  # Highlighted border
  geom_sf(data = border_buffer, fill = "black", color = "black", size= 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  
   # Focus on border region
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-125, -92),  # Adjust longitude range as needed
    ylim = c(22, 42),     # Adjust latitude range as needed
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Spatial Distribution of Hot and Cold Spots Map",
       subtitle = "Age-Standardized Rate, Getis-Ord statistic",
       fill = "Hotspot Type"
       ) + 
      theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 20)), 
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.key.width = unit(1.5, "cm"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm"),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
    ) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5, nrow = 1))

print(getisrdg_map)

ggsave("asmr_getisrdg_map.png", getisrdg_map, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white")


######################################################################################
#Categories of Age-standardized rate
######################################################################################

# Load the RData file
load("joined_asdmr_map_county_data.RData")

data<- joined_asdmr_map_county_data

# Filter for border states
# US border states: California, Arizona, New Mexico, Texas
# Mexico border states: Baja California, Sonora, Chihuahua, Coahuila, Nuevo León, Tamaulipas

border_states_us <- c("California", "Arizona", "New Mexico", "Texas")
border_states_mx <- c("Baja California", "Sonora", "Chihuahua", "Coahuila", 
                      "Nuevo León", "Tamaulipas")

# Filter data for border region
border_data <- data %>%
  filter(STATE_NAME %in% c(border_states_us, border_states_mx))

# Create categories for mapping
border_data <- border_data %>%
  mutate(
    # Clean crude rate variable (handle any text/character issues)
    asmr_clean = as.numeric(asmr_per_100k),
    # Create rate categories (diabetes mortality per 100,000)
    rate_category = case_when(
      asmr_clean < 25 ~ "< 25 (Low)",
      asmr_clean >= 25 & asmr_clean < 50 ~ "25-50 (Moderate)",
      asmr_clean >= 50 & asmr_clean < 100 ~ "50-100 (High)",
      asmr_clean >= 100 & asmr_clean < 150 ~ "100-150 (Very High)",
      asmr_clean >= 150 ~ "≥ 150 (Extremely High)",
      TRUE ~ "No Data"
    ),
    # Create border status
    # border_status = ifelse(is_us_border == 1, "Border County", "Non-Border County"),
    # Country indicator
    country_clean = case_when(
      COUNTRY == "US" | COUNTRY == "USA" ~ "United States",
      COUNTRY == "MX" | COUNTRY == "Mexico" ~ "Mexico",
      TRUE ~ COUNTRY
    )
  )

# Set factor levels for consistent coloring
border_data$rate_category <- factor(border_data$rate_category, 
                                   levels = c("(Very Low)", "< 25 (Low)", "25-50 (Moderate)", "50-100 (High)", 
                                             "100-150 (Very High)", "≥ 150 (Extremely High)", "No Data"))

print(paste("Total counties in border region:", nrow(border_data)))
print("Summary of crude rates:")

border_data %>%
  filter(asmr_clean != 9999) %>%
  pull(asmr_clean) %>%
  summary()

# Summary statistics by country and border status
summary_stats <- border_data %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(!is.na(asmr_clean)) %>%
  group_by(country_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    n_counties = n(),
    mean_rate = round(mean(asmr_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    median_rate = round(median(asmr_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    min_rate = round(min(asmr_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    max_rate = round(max(asmr_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(summary_stats, caption = "Age-std Diabetes Mortality Rate Summary by Country")



# Get country boundaries for US and Mexico
countries <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") %>%
  filter(admin %in% c("United States of America", "Mexico"))

# Get state/province boundaries for both countries
states_us <- ne_states(country = "United States of America", returnclass = "sf")
states_mexico <- ne_states(country = "Mexico", returnclass = "sf")

# Combine state boundaries
all_states <- rbind(
  states_us %>% select(name, admin, geometry),
  states_mexico %>% select(name, admin, geometry)
)

# Create a more precise border line
border_line <- st_intersection(
  st_boundary(countries %>% filter(admin == "United States of America")),
  st_boundary(countries %>% filter(admin == "Mexico"))
)

# Create a buffer around the border line
border_buffer <- st_buffer(border_line, dist = 0.05) 

# Create the map
asdmr_map <- ggplot() +
  # Add country boundaries as base layer
  geom_sf(data = countries, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "gray60", size = 0.3) +
  
  # Add your diabetes data on top
  geom_sf(data = border_data, aes(fill = rate_category), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  
  # ADD STATE BOUNDARIES ON TOP - this is the key addition
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Highlighted border
  geom_sf(data = border_buffer, fill = "black", color = "black", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # scale_fill_viridis_d(
  #   name = "Diabetes Mortality Rate\n(deaths per 100,000)",
  #   option = "plasma",
  #   direction = -1,
  #   na.value = "grey90"
  # ) +
  
    scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Age-standardized Diabetes Mortality Rate\n(deaths per 100,000)",
    values = c(
    "< 25 (Low)" = "#fee5d9",
    "25-50 (Moderate)" = "#fcbba1", 
    "50-100 (High)" = "#fc9272",
    "100-150 (Very High)" = "#fb6a4a",
    "≥ 150 (Extremely High)" = "#de2d26",
    "No Data" = "#fef5f0"
    )) +
  
  # Focus on border region
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-125, -92),  # Adjust longitude range as needed
    ylim = c(22, 42),     # Adjust latitude range as needed
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Age-standardized Diabetes Mortality Rate by County",
    subtitle = "US-Mexico Border Region",
    caption = paste("Data covers", "2018-2023", "| Source: INEGI, CONAPO, CDC")
  ) +
  
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.key.width = unit(1.0, "cm"),
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  ) +
  
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5, nrow = 1))

print(asdmr_map)

ggsave("asmr_county_map.png", asdmr_map, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

###############################
# Jenks Natural Breaks
###############################


# install.packages("classInt")
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(classInt)

# Load the RData file
load("joined_asdmr_map_county_data.RData")
data <- joined_asdmr_map_county_data

# Filter for border states
# US border states: California, Arizona, New Mexico, Texas
# Mexico border states: Baja California, Sonora, Chihuahua, Coahuila, Nuevo León, Tamaulipas

border_states_us <- c("California", "Arizona", "New Mexico", "Texas")
border_states_mx <- c("Baja California", "Sonora", "Chihuahua", "Coahuila", 
                      "Nuevo León", "Tamaulipas")

# Filter data for border region
border_data <- data %>%
  filter(STATE_NAME %in% c(border_states_us, border_states_mx))

border_data <- border_data %>%
  mutate(
    # Clean ASMR variable (handle any text/character issues)
    asmr_clean = as.numeric(asmr_per_100k),
    
    # Country indicator
    country_clean = case_when(
      COUNTRY == "US" | COUNTRY == "USA" ~ "United States",
      COUNTRY == "MX" | COUNTRY == "Mexico" ~ "Mexico",
      TRUE ~ COUNTRY
    )
  )

print(paste("Total counties/municipalities in border region:", nrow(border_data)))
print("\n=== Summary of ASMR (Age-Standardized Mortality Rates) ===")

# Summary statistics by country
summary_stats <- border_data %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(!is.na(asmr_clean) & asmr_clean != 9999) %>%
  group_by(country_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    n_units = n(),
    mean_rate = round(mean(asmr_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    median_rate = round(median(asmr_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    sd_rate = round(sd(asmr_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    min_rate = round(min(asmr_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    max_rate = round(max(asmr_clean, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(summary_stats)

print("\n=== Applying Jenks Natural Breaks Classification ===")

# Remove NA and invalid values for classification
valid_rates <- border_data %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(!is.na(asmr_clean) & asmr_clean != 9999) %>%
  pull(asmr_clean)

print(paste("Valid rates for classification:", length(valid_rates)))

# Apply Jenks Natural Breaks
# n = 5 creates 5 classes (adjust as needed: typically 4-7 work well)
jenks_breaks <- classIntervals(valid_rates, n = 5, style = "jenks")

print("\n=== Jenks Break Points ===")
print(jenks_breaks$brks)

# Create formatted labels showing the rate ranges
n_classes <- length(jenks_breaks$brks) - 1
labels_jenks <- character(n_classes)

for (i in 1:n_classes) {
  lower <- round(jenks_breaks$brks[i], 1)
  upper <- round(jenks_breaks$brks[i+1], 1)
  labels_jenks[i] <- paste0(lower, " - ", upper)
}

print("\n=== Category Labels ===")
print(labels_jenks)

# Assign Jenks categories to data
border_data <- border_data %>%
  mutate(
    rate_category = cut(
      asmr_clean,
      breaks = c(-Inf, jenks_breaks$brks[-1]),
      labels = labels_jenks,
      include.lowest = TRUE
    ),
    # Keep original column for reference
    rate_category_original = case_when(
      asmr_clean < 25 ~ "< 25 (Low)",
      asmr_clean >= 25 & asmr_clean < 50 ~ "25-50 (Moderate)",
      asmr_clean >= 50 & asmr_clean < 100 ~ "50-100 (High)",
      asmr_clean >= 100 & asmr_clean < 150 ~ "100-150 (Very High)",
      asmr_clean >= 150 ~ "≥ 150 (Extremely High)",
      TRUE ~ "No Data"
    )
  )

# Verify categorization
print("\n=== Distribution Across Jenks Categories ===")
print(table(border_data$rate_category, useNA = "ifany"))

print("\n=== Distribution by Country (Jenks Classification) ===")
print(table(border_data$country_clean, border_data$rate_category, useNA = "ifany"))

print("\n=== Loading Geographic Data ===")

# Get country boundaries for US and Mexico
countries <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") %>%
  filter(admin %in% c("United States of America", "Mexico"))

# Get state/province boundaries for both countries
states_us <- ne_states(country = "United States of America", returnclass = "sf")
states_mexico <- ne_states(country = "Mexico", returnclass = "sf")

# Combine state boundaries
all_states <- rbind(
  states_us %>% select(name, admin, geometry),
  states_mexico %>% select(name, admin, geometry)
)

# Create a more precise border line
border_line <- st_intersection(
  st_boundary(countries %>% filter(admin == "United States of America")),
  st_boundary(countries %>% filter(admin == "Mexico"))
)

# Create a buffer around the border line
border_buffer <- st_buffer(border_line, dist = 0.05)

# Create a color palette that matches the number of Jenks classes
# Using a sequential color scheme (oranges/reds - appropriate for mortality data)
color_palette <- c(
  "#fff7f0",  # Very light (low mortality)
  "#fee5d9",
  "#fcbba1",
  "#fc9272",
  "#fb6a4a",
  "#de2d26"   # Dark red (high mortality)
)[1:n_classes]

# Create a named vector for the colors
colors_named <- setNames(color_palette, labels_jenks)

print("\n=== Color Palette for Jenks Classes ===")
print(colors_named)

asdmr_map <- ggplot() +
  # Add country boundaries as base layer
  geom_sf(data = countries, fill = "white", color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Add state boundaries
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "gray60", size = 0.3) +
  
  # Add diabetes data with Jenks classification
  geom_sf(data = border_data, aes(fill = rate_category), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  
  # Add state boundaries on top for clarity
  geom_sf(data = all_states, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Highlighted border
  geom_sf(data = border_buffer, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Apply Jenks-based color scale
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Age-standardized Diabetes\nMortality Rate\n(deaths per 100,000)",
    values = colors_named,
    na.value = "#f5f5f5",
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # Focus on border region
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-125, -92),
    ylim = c(22, 42),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Age-standardized Diabetes Mortality Rate by County/Municipality",
    subtitle = "US-Mexico Border Region",
    caption = paste("Data covers 2018-2023 | Classification: Jenks Natural Breaks | Source: INEGI, CONAPO, CDC WONDER")
  ) +
  
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 5)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 5), color = "gray40"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.key.width = unit(1.0, "cm"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.margin = margin(t = 10),
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "cm"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  ) +
  
  guides(fill = guide_legend(
    title.position = "top",
    title.hjust = 0.5,
    nrow = 2,
    label.position = "bottom",
    label.hjust = 0.5
  ))

print(asdmr_map)

ggsave("asmr_county_map_jenks.png", 
       asdmr_map, 
       width = 10, 
       height = 7, 
       dpi = 300, 
       bg = "white")

print("\n✓ Map saved as 'asmr_county_map_jenks.png'")



```


#### Tables
```{r}

################################################################################
# LISA & GETIS-ORD SPATIAL ANALYSIS REPORTING SCRIPT
# For Diabetes Mortality: US-Mexico Border States
# 
# This script extracts counts and complementary statistics from LISA and 
# Getis-Ord spatial analyses to support publication
################################################################################

library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(spdep)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# Assumes you have already run:
# - filtered_data_ct with spatial analysis results
# - lisa_category, hotspot, local_moran_i, local_moran_p, getis_ord calculated

################################################################################
# SECTION 1: LISA CLUSTER COUNTS & SUMMARIES
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SECTION 1: LISA CLUSTER ANALYSIS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# 1.1 Overall LISA counts
cat("1.1 Overall LISA Category Distribution\n")
lisa_counts <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(LISA_simple) %>%
  summarise(
    n_units = n(),
    n_significant = sum(significance == "Significant"),
    percent = round((n() / nrow(filtered_data_ct)) * 100, 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor(LISA_simple, 
    levels = c("High-High", "Low-Low", "High-Low", "Low-High", "Not significant")))

print(kable(lisa_counts, 
  caption = "Distribution of Counties/Municipalities by LISA Cluster Category",
  format = "simple"))

# 1.2 LISA by country
cat("\n1.2 LISA Categories by Country\n")
lisa_by_country <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, LISA_simple) %>%
  summarise(
    n_units = n(),
    percent_within_country = round((n() / nrow(filter(filtered_data_ct, COUNTRY == COUNTRY[1]))) * 100, 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = LISA_simple,
    values_from = n_units,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  arrange(COUNTRY)

print(kable(lisa_by_country, 
  caption = "LISA Cluster Distribution by Country",
  format = "simple"))

# 1.3 LISA by state
cat("\n1.3 LISA Categories by State\n")
lisa_by_state <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  group_by(STATE_NAME, COUNTRY) %>%
  summarise(
    Total = n(),
    `High-High` = sum(LISA_simple == "High-High"),
    `Low-Low` = sum(LISA_simple == "Low-Low"),
    `High-Low` = sum(LISA_simple == "High-Low"),
    `Low-High` = sum(LISA_simple == "Low-High"),
    `Not Sig.` = sum(LISA_simple == "Not significant"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(COUNTRY, STATE_NAME)

print(kable(lisa_by_state, 
  caption = "LISA Cluster Distribution by State",
  format = "simple"))

################################################################################
# SECTION 2: GETIS-ORD HOTSPOT COUNTS & SUMMARIES
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SECTION 2: GETIS-ORD HOTSPOT ANALYSIS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# 2.1 Overall Getis-Ord counts
cat("2.1 Overall Getis-Ord Hotspot Distribution\n")
hotspot_counts <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(hotspot) %>%
  summarise(
    n_units = n(),
    percent = round((n() / nrow(filtered_data_ct)) * 100, 2),
    mean_rate = round(mean(`asmr_per_100k`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    median_rate = round(median(`asmr_per_100k`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    sd_rate = round(sd(`asmr_per_100k`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor(hotspot,
    levels = c("Hot Spot (95%)", "Hot Spot (80%)", "Not Significant", 
               "Cold Spot (80%)", "Cold Spot (95%)")))

print(kable(hotspot_counts, 
  caption = "Distribution of Counties/Municipalities by Getis-Ord Hotspot Category",
  format = "simple"))

# 2.2 Getis-Ord by country
cat("\n2.2 Getis-Ord Categories by Country\n")
hotspot_by_country <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY, hotspot) %>%
  summarise(
    n_units = n(),
    percent_within_country = round((n() / nrow(filter(filtered_data_ct, COUNTRY == COUNTRY[1]))) * 100, 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = hotspot,
    values_from = n_units,
    values_fill = 0
  )

print(kable(hotspot_by_country, 
  caption = "Getis-Ord Hotspot Distribution by Country",
  format = "simple"))

# 2.3 Getis-Ord by state
cat("\n2.3 Getis-Ord Categories by State\n")
hotspot_by_state <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(STATE_NAME, COUNTRY) %>%
  summarise(
    Total = n(),
    `Hot (95%)` = sum(hotspot == "Hot Spot (95%)"),
    `Hot (80%)` = sum(hotspot == "Hot Spot (80%)"),
    `Not Sig.` = sum(hotspot == "Not Significant"),
    `Cold (80%)` = sum(hotspot == "Cold Spot (80%)"),
    `Cold (95%)` = sum(hotspot == "Cold Spot (95%)"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(COUNTRY, STATE_NAME)

print(kable(hotspot_by_state, 
  caption = "Getis-Ord Hotspot Distribution by State",
  format = "simple"))

################################################################################
# SECTION 3: CROSS-TABULATION: LISA × GETIS-ORD
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SECTION 3: CROSS-TABULATION: LISA × GETIS-ORD\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cross_tab <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(lisa_category, hotspot) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = hotspot,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor(lisa_category, 
    levels = c("High-High", "Low-Low", "High-Low", "Low-High", "Not significant")))

print(kable(cross_tab, 
  caption = "Cross-Tabulation: LISA Clusters vs Getis-Ord Hotspots",
  format = "simple"))

################################################################################
# SECTION 4: GLOBAL SPATIAL AUTOCORRELATION
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SECTION 4: GLOBAL SPATIAL AUTOCORRELATION\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# This assumes you already have: moran_test
# If not, re-run:
# coords <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(filtered_data_ct, of_largest_polygon = TRUE))
# nb <- knn2nb(knearneigh(coords, k = 3))
# weights <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")
# moran_test <- moran.test(filtered_data_ct$`Crude Rate`, weights, 
#                          zero.policy = TRUE, na.action = na.omit)

# Extract Moran's I summary
moran_summary <- data.frame(
  Statistic = c("Moran's I", "Expected I", "Variance", "Z-score", "p-value"),
  Value = c(
    round(moran_test$estimate[1], 4),
    round(moran_test$estimate[2], 4),
    round(moran_test$estimate[3], 4),
    round((moran_test$estimate[1] - moran_test$estimate[2]) / sqrt(moran_test$estimate[3]), 4),
    round(moran_test$p.value, 4)
  )
)

cat("Global Moran's I Test Results (k=3 nearest neighbors):\n")
print(kable(moran_summary, format = "simple"))
cat("\nInterpretation: Moran's I ranges from -1 (perfect dispersion) to +1 (perfect clustering).\n")
if (moran_test$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("Result: Significant positive spatial clustering detected (p < 0.05)\n\n")
} else {
  cat("Result: No significant spatial clustering detected (p ≥ 0.05)\n\n")
}

################################################################################
# SECTION 5: DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS BY CLUSTER TYPE
################################################################################

cat("========================================\n")
cat("SECTION 5: MORTALITY RATES BY CLUSTER TYPE\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# 5.1 Descriptive stats by LISA category
cat("5.1 Crude Mortality Rate Statistics by LISA Category\n")
descriptive_lisa <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(lisa_category) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    Mean = round(mean(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    SD = round(sd(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Median = round(median(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Q1 = round(quantile(`Crude Rate`, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Q3 = round(quantile(`Crude Rate`, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Min = round(min(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Max = round(max(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor(lisa_category, 
    levels = c("High-High", "Low-Low", "High-Low", "Low-High", "Not significant")))

print(kable(descriptive_lisa, 
  caption = "Crude Mortality Rate Statistics by LISA Cluster Category (per 100,000)",
  format = "simple"))

# 5.2 Descriptive stats by Getis-Ord category
cat("\n5.2 Crude Mortality Rate Statistics by Getis-Ord Category\n")
descriptive_hotspot <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(hotspot) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    Mean = round(mean(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    SD = round(sd(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Median = round(median(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Q1 = round(quantile(`Crude Rate`, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Q3 = round(quantile(`Crude Rate`, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Min = round(min(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Max = round(max(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor(hotspot,
    levels = c("Hot Spot (95%)", "Hot Spot (80%)", "Not Significant", 
               "Cold Spot (80%)", "Cold Spot (95%)")))

print(kable(descriptive_hotspot, 
  caption = "Crude Mortality Rate Statistics by Getis-Ord Category (per 100,000)",
  format = "simple"))

################################################################################
# SECTION 6: LOCAL MORAN'S I STATISTICS
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SECTION 6: LOCAL MORAN'S I STATISTICS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# 6.1 Local Moran's I summary
local_moran_summary <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  summarise(
    `N Units` = n(),
    `Mean Local I` = round(mean(local_moran_i, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    `SD Local I` = round(sd(local_moran_i, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    `Min Local I` = round(min(local_moran_i, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    `Max Local I` = round(max(local_moran_i, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    `Sig. p < 0.05 (%)` = paste0(round((sum(local_moran_p < 0.05) / n()) * 100, 1), "%"),
    `Sig. p < 0.10 (%)` = paste0(round((sum(local_moran_p < 0.10) / n()) * 100, 1), "%")
  )

cat("Local Moran's I Summary Statistics:\n")
print(kable(t(local_moran_summary), col.names = "Value", format = "simple"))

# 6.2 Distribution of p-values
cat("\n6.2 Distribution of Local Moran's I p-values\n")
pvalue_dist <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  summarise(
    `p < 0.001` = sum(local_moran_p < 0.001),
    `0.001-0.01` = sum(local_moran_p >= 0.001 & local_moran_p < 0.01),
    `0.01-0.05` = sum(local_moran_p >= 0.01 & local_moran_p < 0.05),
    `0.05-0.10` = sum(local_moran_p >= 0.05 & local_moran_p < 0.10),
    `p ≥ 0.10` = sum(local_moran_p >= 0.10)
  )

print(kable(t(pvalue_dist), col.names = "Count", format = "simple"))

################################################################################
# SECTION 7: GETIS-ORD Gi* Z-SCORE STATISTICS
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SECTION 7: GETIS-ORD Gi* Z-SCORE STATISTICS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

getis_ord_summary <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  summarise(
    `N Units` = n(),
    `Mean Z-score` = round(mean(getis_ord, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    `SD Z-score` = round(sd(getis_ord, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    `Min Z-score` = round(min(getis_ord, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    `Max Z-score` = round(max(getis_ord, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    `Z > 1.96 (Hot 95%)` = sum(getis_ord > 1.96),
    `1.28 < Z ≤ 1.96 (Hot 80%)` = sum(getis_ord > 1.28 & getis_ord <= 1.96),
    `-1.28 < Z ≤ 1.28 (Not Sig.)` = sum(getis_ord >= -1.28 & getis_ord <= 1.28),
    `-1.96 < Z ≤ -1.28 (Cold 80%)` = sum(getis_ord > -1.96 & getis_ord <= -1.28),
    `Z < -1.96 (Cold 95%)` = sum(getis_ord < -1.96)
  )

cat("Getis-Ord Gi* Z-score Summary:\n")
print(kable(t(getis_ord_summary), col.names = "Count", format = "simple"))

################################################################################
# SECTION 8: US vs MEXICO COMPARISON
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SECTION 8: COUNTRY COMPARISON (US vs MEXICO)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

country_comparison <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(COUNTRY) %>%
  summarise(
    `N Units` = n(),
    `Mean Rate` = round(mean(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `Median Rate` = round(median(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `SD Rate` = round(sd(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `High-High (%)` = round((sum(lisa_category == "High-High") / n()) * 100, 1),
    `Low-Low (%)` = round((sum(lisa_category == "Low-Low") / n()) * 100, 1),
    `Sig. Clusters (%)` = round((sum(significance == "Significant") / n()) * 100, 1),
    `Hot Spots (%)` = round((sum(hotspot == "Hot Spot (95%)") / n()) * 100, 1),
    `Cold Spots (%)` = round((sum(hotspot == "Cold Spot (95%)") / n()) * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(kable(country_comparison, 
  caption = "Spatial Clustering Patterns: US vs Mexico Border Regions",
  format = "simple"))

################################################################################
# SECTION 9: COMPREHENSIVE SUMMARY TABLE
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SECTION 9: COMPREHENSIVE SUMMARY TABLE\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

comprehensive_summary <- filtered_data_ct %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(lisa_category) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    `(%)` = round((n() / nrow(filtered_data_ct)) * 100, 1),
    `Mean Rate` = round(mean(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `Median Rate` = round(median(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `Range` = paste0(
      round(min(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 0), "-",
      round(max(`Crude Rate`, na.rm = TRUE), 0)
    ),
    `Sig. p<0.05 (%)` = round((sum(significance == "Significant") / n()) * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor(lisa_category, 
    levels = c("High-High", "Low-Low", "High-Low", "Low-High", "Not significant")))

print(kable(comprehensive_summary, 
  caption = "Comprehensive Summary: LISA Clusters, Frequencies, and Mortality Rates",
  format = "simple"))

################################################################################
# SECTION 10: KEY FINDINGS SUMMARY
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("KEY FINDINGS SUMMARY\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

total_units <- nrow(filtered_data_ct)
high_high <- sum(filtered_data_ct$lisa_category == "High-High")
low_low <- sum(filtered_data_ct$lisa_category == "Low-Low")
significant <- sum(filtered_data_ct$significance == "Significant")
hot_95 <- sum(filtered_data_ct$hotspot == "Hot Spot (95%)")
cold_95 <- sum(filtered_data_ct$hotspot == "Cold Spot (95%)")

cat("• Total counties/municipalities:", total_units, "\n")
cat("• Significant spatial clustering (p < 0.05):", significant, 
    paste0("(", round(significant/total_units*100, 1), "%)"), "\n")
cat("• High-High clusters (hotspots):", high_high, 
    paste0("(", round(high_high/total_units*100, 1), "%)"), "\n")
cat("• Low-Low clusters (coldspots):", low_low, 
    paste0("(", round(low_low/total_units*100, 1), "%)"), "\n")
cat("• Getis-Ord hotspots (95%):", hot_95, 
    paste0("(", round(hot_95/total_units*100, 1), "%)"), "\n")
cat("• Getis-Ord coldspots (95%):", cold_95, 
    paste0("(", round(cold_95/total_units*100, 1), "%)"), "\n")

# Country-specific
us_high_high <- sum(filtered_data_ct$COUNTRY == "US" & filtered_data_ct$lisa_category == "High-High")
mx_high_high <- sum(filtered_data_ct$COUNTRY == "MX" & filtered_data_ct$lisa_category == "High-High")
us_n <- sum(filtered_data_ct$COUNTRY == "US")
mx_n <- sum(filtered_data_ct$COUNTRY == "MX")

cat("\n• US border: High-High clusters:", us_high_high, 
    paste0("of", us_n, "units"), "\n")
cat("• Mexico border: High-High clusters:", mx_high_high, 
    paste0("of", mx_n, "units"), "\n")

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("END OF REPORT\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

################################################################################
# OPTIONAL: Export all tables to Excel workbook
################################################################################

cat("To export all tables to Excel, uncomment the following code:\n\n")
cat("# library(writexl)\n")
cat("# 
# write_xlsx(
#   list(
#     'LISA Counts' = lisa_counts,
#     'Getis-Ord Counts' = hotspot_counts,
#     'LISA by Country' = lisa_by_country,
#     'Hotspot by Country' = hotspot_by_country,
#     'Comprehensive Summary' = comprehensive_summary,
#     'Country Comparison' = country_comparison
#   ),
#   path = 'Spatial_Analysis_Summary_Tables.xlsx'
# )\n\n")


```
